title: Interviewing with Equinix India for Staff Engineer role
content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently interviewing with Equinix for a Staff Engineer / Staff Backend Engineer role in Bangalore.

My current compensation is around:

27L base
10% variable
~1.25L yearly stock
YOE: 5.5+ years
Wanted to understand:

What compensation range is realistic for Staff level at Equinix India?
How much fixed vs RSU split do they usually give?
How is the engineering culture and growth at Staff level?
Is the role truly Staff-level in terms of architecture ownership and impact?

Would really appreciate insights from anyone working at Equinix or who has interviewed recently.

Thanks!

title: Salesforce AMTS Interview || Should I move?
content:
I have recently got a chance to appear in Salesforce AMTS interview process.
It consisted of 3 rounds which were 2 technical and 1 manager round. 

In first round they asked about the `parking lot` design.

In second one DSA question which was focused on linked list and two-pointer, I jumped directly to optimal solution, explained multiple cases, dry-run examples. (It was language independent), after that designing `book my show` with concurrent user try to book a seat.

Both rounds were mainly focused on devops side like:
1. `Containers`
2. `K8`
3. `Deploying conatiners in K8`
4. `DNS and what is meant by DNS server, how to check which server was being targeted what was its IP when we send request in detail`
5. `How to check error and maintain a CI/CD pipeline`
6. `From resume like dictionary (python)`

Since both interviewer were seniors with 15+ years of experience so they focused more on Devops rather than DSA, and the OA was `medium-hard`.

I have asked specification about the role which I am here for and got good response from both and I understood what the role is for.

The hardest was manager round, where it was more about:
1. `Leadership`
2. `What you mean by Trust`
3. `What was my thinking when I choosed B. Tech in CSE`
4. `Family and current work`
5. `Since I am at startup now, it was a bit tricky to explain that thier was no methodoloy like Agile or any thing, but it was their on previous internship`
6. `Indepth analyzing of current company, role, conflict (STAR)`
7. `What would you do if you are not hired: My response was initially I will be a bit-down but till evening I go back to my usual griding`

The first 2 rounds verdict was `good hire`, got call from HR about the timing of the interviews after clearing the first technical round.

But after last round I didn't hear any words, and the questions were not much of technical so I find it really hard and was not able to communicate and express my thoughts, I was transparent what I did, what I want to be, here the question which I asked was `after how long I can own a product` but he said `thats now how it works here you may own tool but more specifically the teams own that, and you will not own a component fully`. I felt akward after asking this question.

I have messeaged HR (via message app not via Gmail as I don't have that) after 2 hour has passed from last round but I didn't hear from them, so I am unsure what is going to happen, as I feel like it could have been a lot better.

Any idea what things to do from now, should I focus on other things or should I do a follow-up.

How I got this oppurtunity?
1. Applied via carrer portal after seeing the role on LinkedIn, (don't know the Job Id as it has been filled)
2. Solved OA with in half of the time allocated as they were mostly `graph` and `dp` based, which I have been prepared as I practise DSA by filtering problem based on acceptance rate at letcode and solve hard problems below `40%` and highly discussed hard problems.
3. I got a mail and Job Description.

About me:
1. I graduated at 2025 from tier 3.
2. Have done an internship at MnC during my 7th sem.
3. Joined a startup on April 2026, as it was to fill my gap
4. Have good understanding of high-level and low-level designing.
5. Solved 300+ hard problems on LeetCode out os 1500+ solved probelms.
6. Project included `Autonomous Meeting Scheduer` utilized multiple `AI Agents` and a simple app similar to `Book My Show`.
7. Have contributed to YouTube extension `(OpenSource)`.
8. Global rank under 400 at some bi-weekly contest.
9. Tech: `AWS`, `Docker`, `CI/CD`, `Linux`, `Java`, `C++`, `Python`, `React`, `Node`


Should I wait for a reply or try other company as current company is bond based and need to exist as soon as possible. I am trying to switch as financially it is causing some problem and had to depend on my parents.

title: Redhat | TCS | Endava | Capgemini
content:
Hi all, I am a 2026 batch student, currently have 4 offers in hand, as the title suggests the companies, I will mention the roles as well:
- Trainee Associate Software Engineer - Redhat
- C1 Systems Engineer - TCS (Digital)
- Associate (Role yet to be assigned after joining mostly either SDE or Data Engineer) - Endava
- Analyst/A4 - Capgemini

My choice is more inclined towards Redhat (as its a product based company and good global company to start my career with). Would be grateful to hear any suggestions or feedback from people who have worked in these companies. Also, had one more question, the Redhat role is an apprenticeship and they did not explicitely mention yet that I will get PPO after it, so does it actually get converted ?

Background:
- Knight@LC
- Built some good useful projects using MERN Stack and AI Integration
- Have prior startup internship experience
- Solved over 1800 problems on LC
- Have a decent resume
- Still applying but getting rejected as I am still not graduated
- College - Tier 2

title: Conde Nast vs Hashedin By Deloitte
content:
I have offers from Conde Nast and Hashedin By Deloitte. 

YOE - 2.2 years FTE + 3 Months Internship  
2024 Passout  
Current Role - Member of Technical Staff - 1 (Backend, Data, and some AI Engineering)

### Hashedin By Deloitte
Position - SDE-II  

Offered Compensation:
- Base - 8.5 LPA
- Performance Bonus - 1.5 LPA
- Other Bonus - 2L (Need to repay if I leave within 18 months)
- Standard Deloitte Benefits

Total CTC - 10.08 LPA (Excluding Other Bonus)

### Conde Nast
Position - L2 (Recommendations Team)

Offered Range - 12.5 to 13.5 LPA including 10% performance bonus (Not finalized yet) + Conde Nast benefits.

I’m not sure about the WLB, career growth, and learning curve at both companies. Which one would you suggest going with?

I’m personally leaning toward Conde Nast.

Also, how can I negotiate better with Conde Nast?

title: Kotak 811 SDE 2 | 4 YOE | Need Help Evaluating & Negotiating Offer
content:
Hey community 👋
Cleared all technical rounds at Kotak 811 SDE-2. Looking for advice from folks who've been through this recently.

My Profile:

YOE: 4 years at a US-based MNC
Current Fixed: ₹21 LPA
Current Bonus: 15% variable (~₹3.15L)
ESOPs: ₹6L over 4 years (~₹1.5L/year vested)
Perks: Free cab, free food, gym reimbursement, home office + WiFi (~₹2L worth)
Effective Total CTC: ~₹27 LPA


Questions I need help with:

1. I've heard Kotak gives a max 30% hike — is that 30% on fixed or total CTC?
2. What fixed salary should I push for realistically? 
3. What does the deferred cash / performance bonus component typically look like at Kotak for 4 YOE?
4. What benefits/perks does Kotak 811 actually offer? (cab, food, insurance, WFH allowance, etc.) — trying to understand the real in-hand difference since I'm giving up ~₹2L in perks.
5. Is there any room to negotiate a joining bonus to compensate for unvested ESOPs I'll be leaving on the table?


Any inputs from Kotak 811 employees or folks who recently went through HR at Kotak would be super helpful! 🙏

title: Just explored this clean breakdown of Machine Learning fundamentals!
content:
🔹 WHAT is ML?

ML Models
Supervised vs Unsupervised
Classification vs Regression
 🔹 HOW does it work?

Data is key!
ML Workflow
Underfitting vs Overfitting
Bias vs Variance

🔹 WHY use ML?

Why Machine Learning matters
Is ML a silver bullet? (Spoiler: not always!)
This structure makes it so easy to grasp the core concepts.

What's your take? Are there any topics you'd add to this list? 🤔

title: Microsoft | SDE2 (L62) | Hyderabad
content:
Education: Tier 3, CSE
Years of Experience: 5
Current CTC: 39 fixed + 3.9 PB + 56k$/4 years (57L)
Prior Experience - FAANG+

Microsoft Offer
Salary: 43,00,000
Stock bonus: USD 100,000 (4 years 25% each year)
Performance Bonus: Upto 20%
Sign-on bonus: 6,00,000 + 6,00,000(RB 2nd year)
Relocation bonus: 3,50,000
Total comp (Salary + bonus + Stock):

1st year: 80.5 LPA (Average case with 10% PB)
2nd year: 77 LPA
3rd and 4th : 72 LPA

Should have negotiated on stocks - could have gone upto 120k$
Also on joining bonus - heard it goes till 16L for 2 years

Advice: Negotiate better - could not push further since had no competing offers.
